Publish clear guidelines on where AI is used, including grading, advising, and content generation (Academic Integrity with AI - Honesty)
Require students to disclose AI use in coursework (Academic Integrity with AI - Honesty)
Provide understandable summaries of algorithmic logic behind AI-based evaluation tools (Academic Integrity with AI - Honesty)
Conduct regular accuracy and bias audits of AI tools (Academic Integrity with AI - Trust)
Communicate results openly and accessibly to faculty, staff, and students (Academic Integrity with AI - Trust)
Offer training so faculty and students understand how AI influences learning and assessment (Academic Integrity with AI - Trust)
Set and follow consistent expectations for AI use in courses (Academic Integrity with AI - Trust)
Conduct formal bias audits for all educational AI systems (Academic Integrity with AI - Fairness)
Diversify datasets used to train algorithms (Academic Integrity with AI - Fairness)
Ensure equitable access to AI tools across socioeconomic backgrounds (Academic Integrity with AI - Fairness)
Provide alternative ways for students to demonstrate learning that are not based solely on AI-generated evaluations (Academic Integrity with AI - Fairness)
Treat AI as a supportive tool rather than a replacement for human expertise (Academic Integrity with AI - Respect)
Protect student privacy by following data protection standards (Academic Integrity with AI - Respect)
Teach students to respect intellectual contributions by citing AI-assisted work when appropriate (Academic Integrity with AI - Respect)
Encourage active engagement, reflection, and discussion even when AI offers easier pathways (Academic Integrity with AI - Respect)
Develop and regularly update clear policies on acceptable AI use (Academic Integrity with AI - Responsibility)
Educate students about academic integrity in the age of AI (Academic Integrity with AI - Responsibility)
Monitor for misconduct such as AI-generated plagiarism or overdependence (Academic Integrity with AI - Responsibility)
Encourage students to use AI tools ethically, with proper attribution and awareness of risks (Academic Integrity with AI - Responsibility)
Encourage faculty, staff, and students to speak openly when AI practices conflict with institutional values (Academic Integrity with AI - Courage)
Reward integrity-driven decisions even when they require extra time or resources (Academic Integrity with AI - Courage)
Pilot new AI tools gradually to identify ethical or equity concerns before wide implementation (Academic Integrity with AI - Courage)
Promote a campus culture where thoughtful experimentation and honest feedback are welcomed (Academic Integrity with AI - Courage)
